title: Hello, world
text: This is the first post on my blog. I’m Ray, a data scientist at Wallick Communities, and this site is where I’ll be writing about what I’m learning — mostly Python, SQL, and bits of the wider data ecosystem.

title: Hello, world
text:
What to expect
A few categories of posts I’m planning:

Notes from learning Python — small things I had to figure out the hard way.
Working with data — patterns I find useful for reports and analytics work.
Tooling — the editors, libraries, and workflows I’ve settled on (and the ones I bounced off of).

If a post helps you, I’d love to hear about it.

title: Hello, world
section: A code example, because every dev blog needs one
text:
A code example, because every dev blog needs one
def greet(name: str) -> str:
    return f"Hello, {name}!"

print(greet("world"))
More soon.

title: Ray Wang
text:
Hey, I’m Ray

Thanks for stopping by!
I work as a data scientist at Wallick Communities, where I focus on reporting, analytics, and turning operational data into something useful for the rest of the business.
Outside of work I’m on an ongoing learning journey through Python, statistics, and modern data tooling. This site is where I write things down — partly so future-me can find them, and partly in case they help someone else.
Get in touch by sending me a note!

title: Ray Wang
text:
About me


Reports and analytics by day, Python learner by night.
My day-to-day involves working with operational data — pulling it together, modeling it, and packaging it into reports and dashboards that help teams make decisions. I enjoy the slow craft of turning messy data into something legible.


Data Scientist ∙ Wallick Communities




I’m always picking up new tools and ideas — currently spending a lot of time with Python, SQL, and the wider data ecosystem. I write up the things I learn (and the things that stumped me) on the blog, and the things I build show up in projects.


Currently learning ∙ Python & data science

title: Accessibility commitment
text: Travel back to the home page or about page.

title: Accessibility commitment
text:
Feedback
I welcome any feedback on the accessibility of this site. Please let me know if you encounter any accessibility barriers by using my contact form and I’ll do my best to respond promptly.
Thank you for visiting and for taking the time to read this page

title: Accessibility commitment
text:
Accessibility practices
This site has been designed with the following features in mind:

A color palette that meets WCAG 2.1 AA standards for contrast
Alternative text for all informative images
Readable font faces, specifically to avoid impostor letter shapes and mirroring. This site primarily uses Red Hat Text, which is freely available and has been found to be relatively accessible to users with dyslexic traits and poor near vision.1
A table of contents in the blog post sidebar for easier navigation

I’m not a web developer, but my plan is to periodically audit this site for accessibility failures and learn how to remedy them as best I can. Tools I find useful:

WAVE Web Accessibility Evaluation Tool

title: Accessibility commitment
text:
Footnotes


For more on font accessibility, I recommend the talk Don’t Believe the Type! by The Readability Group.↩︎
